name: Distill meeting notes with MySQL and Outlook hands-free

options.systemPromptTemplate: You are a precise extraction engine. Only extract values present in the text.

parameters.text: =✅ Distill meeting notes with MySQL and Outlook hands-free — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05677-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Collect Raw Text

### Collect Raw Text

Shapes the item: writes `content`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Extract Structured Fields

### Extract Structured Fields

Pulls structured fields (draftId, topic, audience) out of free text using a strict extraction prompt.

Ports: ①→ All Required Fields Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Extract Structured Fields** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### All Required Fields Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.output?.draftId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Upsert into MySQL · ②→ Queue for Manual Review

### Upsert into MySQL

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Queue for Manual Review

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Merge Review Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty topic

### Guard Non-Empty topic

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (MySQL)

### Append Audit Row (MySQL)

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Distill meeting notes with MySQL and Outlook hands-free

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill meeting notes with MySQL and Outlook hands-free. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual captions is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Collect Raw Text → Extract Structured Fields → All Required Fields Found? → Upsert into MySQL → Merge Review Paths → Guard Non-Empty topic → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating captions in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Groq API key; Microsoft OAuth2; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The required-fields check runs *before* the write, not after — an incomplete extraction goes to manual review instead of landing a half-populated record in the system of record. Upsert (not plain create) means re-processing the same document twice updates the existing record instead of duplicating it.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a new field to the extraction schema and mark it required — watch more documents route to manual review.
2. Feed it a document deliberately missing a required field and trace exactly where it ends up.
3. Change the extraction target (e.g. invoice → contract fields) and adjust the upsert key to match.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your captions process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
